hypothesis: If an LLM-driven agent that selectively uses external tools (short-/long-term memory, periodic table, knowledge base) and iteratively proposes compositions with DiffCSP-generated structures and iComFormer property feedback is employed, then it will achieve higher success rates at finding novel, compositionally valid inorganic materials within target formation-energy windows (and higher V.U.N. scores) than tool-free LLM variants and diffusion-only baselines, because explicit tool use and iterative structure/property evaluation guide more effective materials discovery.
